english: beneficial influence, la (la) on, Glr. - zla-ba rgyás-pa (zla ba rgyas pa) full moon Pth.; nya-rgyás zla-ba (nya rgyas zla ba) id. - rgyás-pai tsá-ba (rgyas pa'i tsha ba), rgyas-tsád (rgyas tshad) n. of a disease Med. - ži-rgyás (zhi rgyas) etc. v. glíṅ (gling), and ži-ba (zhi ba).

I. 1. matter, substance, material, rgyu šél-las (rgyu shel las) crystal being the material; čáṅ-rgyu (chang rgyu) ingredients for making beer, i.e. barley, barm etc.; rgyu dgé-ba bságs-pas tób-pa yin (rgyu dge ba bsags pas thob pa yin) (the human body) is a substance obtained by accumulating virtue Thgy.; ṅá-la dgós-rgyu čuṅ (nga la dgos rgyu chung) I have few wants Mil.; also for substance in an emphatical sense, = nervus rerum, money Mil.; bzó-rgyu (bzo rgyu) matter or substance of which any thing is made or manufactured, material Glr.; zá-rgyu med (za rgyu med) we have nothing to eat Glr.; hence opportunity, chance, possibility, dá-ltar rgyu žig snáṅ-ṅo (da ltar rgyu zhig snang ngo) an opportunity offers now Dzl.; arrangements, preparation, *°dó-gyu jhẹ́-pa* (*'gro rgyu byed pa*) C. to make preparations for a journey. In a special sense: material, stuff for weaving, warp, chain. — 2. cause, reason, motive, main condition, mya-ṅán-las 'dás-pai rgyur gyur (mya ngan las 'das pa'i rgyur gyur) it becomes the cause of Nirwana, i.e. it leads to Nirwana Dzl.; in elliptical speech: lha daṅ mii rgyur gyúr-pai dyé-ba (lha dang mi'i rgyur gyur pa'i dge ba) the virtue that leads to (the re-birth amongst) gods or men Dzl. IV, 17 (Sch. incorr.); in the same manner ṅán-soṅ rgyú-ru 'gro (ngan song rgyu ru 'gro); rgyus (rgyus) c. genit. by reason of, on account of, in consequence of Tar.; číi rgyus (ci'i rgyus) why Stg.; rgyu méd-du (rgyu med du), méd-par (med par) without the impulse of a foreign cause, spontaneously; without sufficient reason, without good cause, the Latin temere; rgyu daṅ rkyen (rgyu dang rkyen) Cs. and Sch. 'cause and effect', more correctly (cf. rkyen (rkyen)): primary and secondary cause, which, certainly, sometimes coincides with 'origin and further development', and so, too, with 'cause and effect'; rgyu daṅ rkyen deï p̱yír (rgyu dang rkyen de'i phyir), deï rgyu deï rkyéṅ-gyis (de'i rgyu de'i rkyen gyis), deï rgyu-rkyéṅ (de'i rgyu rkyen), therefore, on that account; in Med.: nyé-bai rgyu (nye ba'i rgyu) the three anthropological causes or conditions of diseases, the three 'humours', air, bile, and phlegm; ríṅ-bai rgyu (ring ba'i rgyu) the ultimate cause of diseases, and of every evil, viz. ignorance (mu-ríg-pa (mu rig pa), v. ríg-pa (rig pa)); skyéd-byed rgyu (skyed byed rgyu) the creative cause Zam.; 'p̱él-bai rgyu ni lté-ba ('phel ba'i rgyu ni lte ba) the main condition, the efficient cause, of growth is the navel-string Med.; rgyu byéd-pa (rgyu byed pa) to be the principal cause of, to lie at the bottom of a matter Mil.; rgyu skyéd-pa (rgyu skyed pa) to lay the foundation of Dzl. — 3. after verbal roots rgyu (rgyu) implies necessity, like our I am to, I have to, I am obliged to, I ought to; in later literature, as well as in the present col. language of C., it indicates the fut. tense: čós-skor yyás-nas byéd-rgyu-la (chos skor g-yas nas byed rgyu la) whereas the holy circumabulation (v. skor-ba (skor ba) I, 2) ought to be performed from the right (to the left) Mil.; sdáṅ-dgra yál-rgyu-la (sdang dgra yal rgyu la) as the enemy must vanish, or: is sure to come to an end Mil.; *sol-ċóg ṭal-díg jhá-gyu yín-nam* (*gsol lcog gral sgrig bya rgyu yin nam*) C. am I to lay the cloth? *dhá-ta tó-ċa zá-la °dó-gyu yin* (*da lta lto cha za la 'gro rgyu yin*) C. now I will go and dine; ṅai drúṅ-du 'óṅ-rgyu yín-pa (nga'i drung du 'ong rgyu yin pa) those that intended to come to me (the Latin 'venturi') Glr.; dé-la rgyal-sríd ytád-rgyu-la (de la rgyal srid gtad rgyu la) when the government was to be conferred upon him, when he was to enter upon his reign Glr.; rta žóṅ-rgyu med (rta zhon rgyu med) (riding-) horses were not to be had Glr. — When appended to adjectives, it is a mere pleonastical addition: dkóṅ-rgyu med (dkon rgyu med) that is not a very precious thing, there is nothing particular in that Mil.; čúṅ-rgyui lha-káṅ (chung rgyu'i lha khang) a very small temple Mil.; yžáṅ-pas légs-rgyu med (gzhan pas legs rgyu med) he is not more beautiful than others Glr.; ya-mtsáṅ-rgyu-med (ya mtshan rgyu med) that is not to be wondered at; *°gó-gyu man* (*dgos rgyu min*) C. that is useless. Comp. rgyu-rkyéṅ (rgyu rkyen) (v above sub no. 2) connection, meaning, signification, rgyu-rkyen bšad-du gsol (rgyu rkyen bshad du gsol) please explain to me the connection, which is often used in a general sense = what does that mean? what is that? Glr., but also in a special sense relative to law-suits: *kỵó-kyi gyu-kyén žú-la °do* (*khyod kyi rgyu rkyen zhu la 'gro*) C. I am going to tell what it is with you, i.e. I shall inform against you, bring an action against you. — rgyu-čá (rgyu cha) col. that which belongs to a thing, an appurtenance, necessary implement etc. —
